content:
5 min read
Just now

--

For the first time in my career, I felt something close to depression, and the reason was AI.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Created by Grok | Edited by Khurram ShahzadAbout 14 years ago, I thought I had figured out my career. Previously, I was a financial analyst and considered this profession dull, unenergetic, persistent, and demotivating. Why was I thinking this way? I do not know. The only thing was that I was not enjoying it at all.
12 years ago, I got a full-time content writing job, and it was an achievement because I thought I could make a decent living by stringing words together. I have expertise in writing web copy, blog posts, email campaigns, and many others. More interestingly, I loved the craft, strategy, and style, and my growing interest in it: identifying the most relevant topics, choosing the right angle and style, and writing a piece that can engage readers. Initially, I found it less glamorous, but I came to enjoy it.
Now? I am staring at my screen some mornings wondering if I have been fooling myself.
The Moment It Hit Me
The first time AI really hit me was when a client casually mentioned using ChatGPT to “rough out” a draft. What used to take me a full day of research and writing? They had something serviceable in minutes. It was not perfect: clunky in places…
